seoTitle: How to Add Cloudflare to WordPress: Install & Setup
metaDescription: Connect Cloudflare's free CDN to your WordPress site: route nameservers, install the official plugin, and apply recommended performance settings.
intro: Pairing Cloudflare with WordPress gives you a free CDN, added security, and easy performance tuning through the official plugin, but the setup involves both DNS-level and WordPress-level steps. This guide covers adding your domain to Cloudflare, routing nameservers, and connecting the plugin with an API token so the recommended settings apply cleanly.

steps.heading: Add your domain to Cloudflare
steps.body: Enter your WordPress site's root domain into Cloudflare and run the automated DNS scan to import your existing A records.

steps.heading: Route your nameservers
steps.body: Copy the custom nameservers Cloudflare generates and update them at your domain registrar so traffic flows through Cloudflare's network.

steps.heading: Set SSL to Full Strict
steps.body: Configure Full (Strict) SSL in Cloudflare to avoid the redirect loops that commonly happen when WordPress and Cloudflare disagree on how to handle HTTPS.

steps.heading: Install the official Cloudflare plugin
steps.body: In your WordPress admin dashboard, install and activate the official Cloudflare plugin from the plugin directory.

steps.heading: Generate and connect an API token
steps.body: Create an API token in Cloudflare and paste it into the plugin's settings to authenticate the connection between WordPress and your Cloudflare account.

steps.heading: Apply recommended settings
steps.body: Use the plugin's one-click option to apply Cloudflare's recommended performance and security presets for WordPress.

faq.question: Why is my WordPress site stuck in a redirect loop after adding Cloudflare?
faq.answer: This usually happens with a mismatched SSL mode — setting Cloudflare to Full (Strict) instead of Flexible typically resolves it.

faq.question: Do I need the Cloudflare plugin if I already added my domain to Cloudflare?
faq.answer: The plugin isn't strictly required for DNS and proxying to work, but it makes applying WordPress-specific optimizations and clearing cache much easier.

faq.question: Is the Cloudflare CDN for WordPress free?
faq.answer: Yes, the core CDN, SSL, and plugin integration are all available on Cloudflare's free plan.

faq.question: What does the API token do in the plugin?
faq.answer: It authenticates the plugin so it can apply Cloudflare settings and purge cache directly from your WordPress dashboard without you logging into Cloudflare separately.
